summary: Standardized shared ground/air state representation by replacing legacy `GROUND` and `AIR` macros with a typed `GroundOrAir` enum using `GA_Ground` and `GA_Air`. The enum was moved into `include/common_structs.h` so both fighter and item code can share it, and fields such as `Fighter.xE0_ground_or_air` and `SpawnItem.x48_ground_or_air` were typed as `GroundOrAir` instead of `s32`.
what_changed: Removed global `#define GROUND 0` and `#define AIR 1` macros from `include/common_structs.h`.
Added `typedef enum GroundOrAir { GA_Ground, GA_Air } GroundOrAir;` to `include/common_structs.h`.
Removed the anonymous fighter-local enum for `GA_Ground` and `GA_Air` from `src/melee/ft/fighter.h`, consolidating the definition in a shared header.
Updated fighter-side comparisons from `GROUND`/`AIR` to `GA_Ground`/`GA_Air`, including Ness yo-yo collision, PSI Magnet, PK Fire spawn behavior, and collision combo-count logic.
Updated item spawn setup functions to assign `GA_Air` or `GA_Ground` to `SpawnItem.x48_ground_or_air`.
smart_moves: Placed `GroundOrAir` in `common_structs.h` rather than keeping it in `fighter.h`, because the same semantic state is used by both fighters and items.
Preserved numeric values by declaring `GA_Ground` first and `GA_Air` second, matching the previous `GROUND 0` and `AIR 1` constants.
Typed struct fields directly as `GroundOrAir`, documenting intent at the data-layout level without changing field offsets.
Replaced ambiguous all-caps macros with scoped semantic names that are less likely to collide with other code and are easier to grep.
Kept the refactor small and mechanical: only constants, field types, and direct comparisons/assignments changed.
decomp_lessons: When a numeric state is shared across subsystems, prefer one shared enum typedef over duplicate anonymous enums or broad macros.
Typed enums can improve struct documentation while preserving ABI/layout when the original field was an integer-sized state value.
Avoid generic macros like `GROUND` and `AIR`; they are too broad for a large decompilation codebase and obscure which domain owns the values.
For cross-system state fields, update both the producer side and consumer side in the same PR to prevent semantic drift.
A low-risk cleanup can still aid future matching by making branch conditions and action-state decisions more self-documenting.

naming_conventions.meaning: typedef name for a two-value ground/air state shared by fighters and items
enum value replacing legacy `GROUND`; numeric value remains 0
enum value replacing legacy `AIR`; numeric value remains 1
spawn item field now typed as `GroundOrAir`

assembly_or_matching_tactics: No explicit matching discussion was present in the PR text or comments.
The change appears intentionally ABI-neutral: replacing `s32` constants with enum values should compile to the same immediate values for comparisons and assignments.
Keeping `GA_Ground == 0` and `GA_Air == 1` preserves existing branch behavior for ground/air checks.
review_feedback: No review comments or review feedback were included in the provided PR context.
The PR body only states `Resolves #520.`, so the motivation beyond standardization is inferred from the title and diff.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining uses of generic `GROUND` or `AIR` macros after PR #521.
Search for other fields named `*_ground_or_air` that should use `GroundOrAir`.
Check whether item comments for `x48_ground_or_air` should be updated from `stationary` terminology to ground/air terminology.
Look for duplicate ground/air enums or constants in subsystem-local headers.
Review issue #520 for the original rationale behind standardizing ground/air constants.
